name: ResumeRadar — ATS Analyzer

nodes.name: 2. Agent 1 — Resume Parser
nodes.notes: Calls Groq to extract structured resume data.

nodes.name: 3. Agent 2 — JD Parser
nodes.notes: Runs in parallel with Agent 1. Extracts JD requirements.

nodes.name: 4. Merge Outputs
nodes.notes: Combines parsed resume + JD into one payload for analysis agents.

nodes.name: 5. Agent 3 — Gap Analyzer
nodes.notes: The scoring heart. Returns match score, gaps, quick wins.

nodes.name: 6. Agent 4 — Rewriter
nodes.notes: Rewrites bullet points with missing keywords woven in naturally.

nodes.name: 7. Assemble Response
nodes.notes: Merges gap analysis + rewrites into the final JSON response.

nodes.name: 8. Send Response
nodes.notes: Returns the complete analysis to the frontend.

_readme: SETUP INSTRUCTIONS: 1) Import this file into n8n via Settings → Import. 2) In n8n, go to Settings → Environment Variables, add GROQ_API_KEY = your key from console.groq.com. 3) Activate the workflow. 4) Your endpoint is: POST http://localhost:5678/webhook/analyze-resume with JSON body { resume_text: '...', job_description: '...' }. 5) To use this instead of the FastAPI backend, change VITE_API_URL in the frontend .env to the n8n webhook URL.
